content.full_text:
October 18, 1990

Mr. Wayne Imberi  
Campaign Consultant  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street  
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: IUE Committee on Political Education, AFL-CIO - Campaign Contributions

Dear Mr. Imberi:

This letter is to confirm our phone conversation of October 5, 1990 concerning the ability of the IUE Committee on Political Education, AFL-CIO ("IUE-COPE"), a federally registered political action committee, to make financial contributions to California gubernatorial candidates.

As I indicated to you, IUE-COPE has filed a Statement 410 with the FPPC, but has not yet received back our notice of registration. You explained that this is due to a backlog of registrations. You advised me, however, that IUE-COPE may, nonetheless, at this time take advantage of the recent federal court rulings striking down California's campaign contribution limits and may make unlimited contributions in the gubernatorial race. If my understanding of this opinion is incorrect, please contact me at the above address or telephone number.

Thank you very much.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
Lauren Rothfarb  
Political Action Representative

cc: William H. Bywater  
Ed Fire  
William Gray  
Maria Landolfo
